title: [Screenful app] Analytics 2 now available to all Monday users 🚀
excerpt: Hey all data aficionados :waving_hand:  I just wanted to let you know that the new Screenful analytics engine, Analytics 2 , is now available to all monday.com users. It is a complete rewrite of our data warehouse, data …

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey all data aficionados I just wanted to let you know that the new Screenful analytics engine, Analytics 2 , is now available to all monday.com users. It is a complete rewrite of our data warehouse, data model, data retrieval algorithms, and APIs. With Analytics 2, we aim to bring you the most flexible and scalable analytics for monday.com data.

The new analytics capabilities include:

  • Create charts using Formula columns
  • Track work by any Date or Timeline column
  • Improved Mirror column handling
  • Time tracking report template
  • Chart click-throughs
screenful.com

Analytics 2 is now available to all monday.com users 🚀 - Screenful Blog

Screenful Analytics 2 enables flexible and scalable analytics for monday.com data Explore the latest updates in the Screenful release, enhancing your project management capabilities and driving team collaboration

If you have any analytics and reporting needs that you could consider solving with an add-on, let us know!

posts.author: arf (Alex R-F)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please consider adding the word ‘Add-On’ or ‘App’ to the title of this post. As it stands, it’s disingenuous.

This is not native functionality, it is an app that costs money and involves third-party data processing. Saying it’s “available to all Monday users” strongly implies that it’s a new feature released by Monday.

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml: Good point. We don’t want to confuse the native functionality with what is provided by the apps. I have now adjusted the headline to contain the word app to make it more explicit.

posts.author: arf (Alex R-F)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you! FWIW, this app seems very cool.

(I wish my org could try it out, but we’re subject to HIPAA data protection requirements and therefore cannot use the vast majority of third-party apps.)
